data.title: Crude oil rebounds as Russia suggests OPEC+ may reverse planned production hike
data.description:
Crude oil futures rose sharply on Friday but still finished with another week of losses, as markets were pushed and pulled by OPEC's decision to go ahead with production increases starting in April, uncertainty over U.S. tariffs, and a bearish build in U.S. crude stocks.

Friday's gains followed remarks by Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak that OPEC+ could reverse the production increase planned to begin next month, as the group will go ahead with the April increase but may then consider other steps including reducing production.

If there is an imbalance in the market, "we can always play in the other direction," Novak said, according to Reuters.

But prices pulled back from session highs after President Trump threatened sanctions on Russia if it fails to reach a ceasefire deal with Ukraine, saying in a post on Truth Social that he was "strongly considering large-scale sanctions" on Russian banks and tariffs on Russian products because its armed forces continue attacks in Ukraine.

Trump's comments appear to contradict reports earlier this week that the U.S. administration is drawing up plans to potentially provide sanctions relief for Russia as part of its plan to end the war and improve U.S.-Russia diplomatic and economic relations.

Then Bloomberg reported that U.S. officials were weighing easing the price cap on Russian oil if there is progress in talks over the war in Ukraine.

Other news, including delays in seeking a permanent ceasefire between Israel and Hamas in Gaza, were "overwhelmed by Russia news," Price Futures Group analyst Phil Flynn said. "It's all Russia, Russia, Russia."

Crude also found support Friday from a weakening dollar and U.S. plans to refill its strategic oil reserve, but WTI still stretched its losing streak to seven weeks.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for April delivery settled -3.9% to $67.04/bbl this week, while front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for May delivery ended -3.3% to $70.36/bbl for the week; on Friday, WTI climbed 1% and Brent jumped 1.3%.

U.S. natural gas enjoyed its most bullish week in two months, with the front-month Nymex April (NG1:COM) contract closing +14.7% this week at $4.399/MMBtu, its second-highest settlement YTD; on Friday, the contract rose 2.2%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

WTI "may be finding some support," Alex Hodes of StoneX said. "Although the highs of the year are likely in the rearview, there appears to be more price risk to the upside than downside at current price levels," seeing bullish risk with reports of OPEC+ rethinking the announced production returns and potential disruption to Iranian supplies.

"While the delay in tariffs has provided a brief sigh of relief, the market is still walking a tightrope between policy uncertainty and oversupply concerns," Rystad Energy’s Mukesh Sahdev wrote. "The tariff threats sent shockwaves through markets, not just because of potential trade disruptions, but also due to fears of retaliatory moves that could ripple through energy supply chains."

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), finished this week -3.9%.

Top 10 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Sibanye Stillwater (SBSW) +21.6%, DRDGold (DRD) +20.6%, Nine Energy Service (NINE) +19.2%, Novagold Resources (NG) +19%, Harmony Gold (HMY) +19%, Lithium Americas (LAC) +16.6%, Array Technologies (ARRY) +16.5%, FuelCell Energy (FCEL) +14.2%, Fortuna Silver Mines (FSM) +13.6%, Gold Fields (GFI) +12.3%.

Top 10 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Venture Global (VG) -38.8%, Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) -29.6%, Kosmos Energy (KOS) -22%, Amplify Energy (AMPY) -21.8%, Vital Energy (VTLE) -21.6%, Chart Industries (GTLS) -21.4%, Oklo (OKLO) -21.3%, Kodiak Gas Services (KGS) -20.7%, Maxeon Solar Technologies (MAXN) -19.5%, 5E Advanced Materials (FEAM) -18.9%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: WhiteHorse Finance emphasizes non-sponsor deals and navigates challenging market conditions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: WhiteHorse Finance (WHF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Stuart Aronson reported disappointing financial results for Q4 2024, citing a decline in the investment portfolio due to net realized and unrealized losses. GAAP net investment income (NII) was $8 million or $0.34 per share, which was below the $0.385 per share quarterly distribution.
  • Net asset value (NAV) per share declined to $12.31, down 3.6% from the prior quarter, impacted by realized losses, markdowns, and a special dividend. Specific markdowns included $2.6 million for American Crafts and $2.2 million for Aspect Software.
  • Gross capital deployments were $35.4 million, offset by $46.2 million in total repayments and sales, resulting in net repayments of $10.8 million. New originations included five sponsor-backed deals and one non-sponsor deal, all first-lien loans.
  • CFO Joyson Thomas highlighted that fee income increased to $0.9 million for Q4 due to non-recurring fees. Risk ratings showed a slight decline in portfolio positions rated 1 or 2, with an increase in 5-rated positions.

Outlook

  • Management indicated that repayment activity is expected to remain high in 2025, with a solid pipeline of about 170 deals. The team has closed five new investments totaling $27.8 million in early 2025.
  • CEO Aronson emphasized a strategic focus on non-sponsor deals, which present better risk-adjusted returns and less competition compared to sponsor deals. The company aims to increase the share of non-sponsor deals in its portfolio moving forward.
  • The lending market remains aggressive, with compressed spreads and higher leverage, particularly in the sponsor segment. Management is cautious about participating in transactions with insufficient cash flow to cover fixed charges.

Financial Results

  • Net investment income of $8 million in Q4 2024 was lower than Q3's $9.2 million. NAV per share decreased to $12.31 from $12.77 in Q3, reflecting special dividends and portfolio markdowns.
  • Non-accrual investments increased to 7.2% of the debt portfolio at fair value, compared to 6.5% in Q3, driven by American Crafts and Aspect Software. The company is working towards restructuring Telestream, a key non-accrual investment, and hopes to return part of it to accrual status by Q2 2025.
  • Weighted average effective yield on income-producing debt investments decreased to 12.5%, down from 13.1% in Q3 2024, primarily due to lower base rates.

Q&A

  • Mickey Schleien, Ladenburg: Asked about the stability of spreads and potential for widening given market uncertainties. CEO Aronson responded that spreads have remained stable but could widen with increased economic disruption or M&A activity.
  • Sean-Paul Adams, Raymond James: Questioned strategies to address non-accrual issues and mitigate risks from tariffs and base rates. CEO Aronson emphasized a focus on first-lien investments and careful underwriting to ensure debt service coverage, avoiding overly leveraged transactions.
  • Melissa Wedel, JPMorgan: Asked about the impact of declining base rates on net investment income and future portfolio repricing. CFO Thomas explained that most of the portfolio resets quarterly or monthly, with ongoing adjustments expected in Q1 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about the rise in non-accruals and market competitiveness, with a slightly negative tone regarding credit quality and income stability.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in their strategic pivot to non-sponsor deals and focus on portfolio stability but acknowledged challenges in the lending market.
  • Compared to Q3, the emphasis on mitigating risks and cautious optimism about new deal flow showed a consistent approach.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Net investment income declined from $9.2 million in Q3 to $8 million in Q4, while NAV per share decreased further due to special dividends and additional markdowns.
  • The proportion of non-accrual investments increased, reflecting challenges with specific portfolio companies such as American Crafts and Aspect Software.
  • Management's tone shifted slightly more towards caution, with a focus on navigating an aggressive market environment and emphasizing non-sponsor opportunities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Rising non-accrual investments, accounting for 7.2% of the debt portfolio, with key concerns around American Crafts, Aspect Software, and Telestream.
  • Aggressive market conditions with compressed spreads and increased leverage, particularly in sponsor-backed deals.
  • Tariff uncertainties and inflationary risks impacting credit quality and underwriting standards.

Final Takeaway

WhiteHorse Finance faced challenges in Q4 2024, with declining NAV and increased non-accruals impacting performance. Management is pivoting strategically towards non-sponsor deals to navigate competitive lending conditions and aims to stabilize the portfolio through cautious underwriting and a focus on first-lien investments. Despite market pressures, a solid pipeline for 2025 provides potential for recovery and growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. steelmakers urge Trump to resist tariff exclusions
data.description:
The CEOs of the three largest U.S. steel producers urged President Trump on Friday to resist requests to exempt steel imports from upcoming 25% tariffs.

Nine steel industry executives including Nucor's (NYSE:NUE) Leon Topalian, US Steel's (X) David Burritt and Cleveland-Cliffs' (CLF) Lourenco Goncalves sent a letter to Trump saying previous exemptions given to nations allowed for increased import volumes that lowered the original impact of the president's levies during his first administration.

"Millions of tons of product-specific exclusions were granted, even for products readily available from domestic suppliers. The result was a weakened U.S. steel industry exposed again to the global steel oversupply crisis," the letter said, according to Bloomberg. "We urge you to resist any requests for exceptions or exclusions and to continue standing strong on behalf of American steel."

The looming threat of tariffs has raised prices for American steel in recent weeks, making it more than 20% more expensive than imported steel; steel was selling for less than $700/ton as recently as January, but by the end of February, domestic producers were said to be quoting customers prices as high as $1,000/ton, a level not seen since the beginning of last year.

High-ranking executives of Commercial Metals (CMC), Metallus (MTUS), North American Stainless, Steel Dynamics (STLD), Tenaris (TS) and Zekelman Industries also signed the letter.

data.title: DoorDash, TKO, Williams-Sonoma, Expand Energy joining S&P 500
data.description:
DoorDash (DASH), WWE’s parent TKO (NYSE:TKO), and two other companies will be added to the S&P 500 (SP500) this month, and four names will drop in exchange. 

High-end housewares retailer Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) and natural gas producer Expand Energy (NASDAQ:EXE) will also enter the benchmark index (SP500), S&P Dow Jones Indices said late Friday. The changes are effective before trading opens March 24. 

At the same time, Borgwarner (NYSE:BWA), Teleflex (NYSE:TFX) and Celanese (NYSE:CE) will exit along with agricultural chemicals company FMC (NYSE:FMC). 

“All companies being added to the S&P 500 are more representative of the large-cap market space,” the index provider said. It’s also making changes in the S&P 100 (SP100), S&P MidCap 400, and the S&P SmallCap 600 indexes. 

S&P 500 (SP500) ETFs include: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

(NYSEARCA:MDY) is among mid-cap ETFs such as: (IJH), (VO), and (SCHM). 

And here are some small-cap ETFs: (NYSEARCA:IJR), (IWM), (VB), (VBR), (VBK), (SCHA), (SPSM), and (DFAS).

data.title: Gold gains for 9th week in 10 on safe-haven demand; China continues gold purchases
data.description:
Gold futures fell modestly on Friday but gained for the week due to safe-haven inflows driven by geopolitical uncertainty, tariff tensions and weak economic growth.

Gold edged lower Friday following the release of the Labor Department report that said the U.S. economy added 151K jobs in February, slightly lower than expected, while the unemployment rate ticked higher to 4.1%, sending the U.S. dollar index tumbling to a four-month low and tallying its steepest weekly decline since November 2022, making greenback-priced bullion less expensive for foreign buyers.

The gold market is currently in a consolidation phase, with safe haven interest providing ongoing support, Zaner Metals senior metals strategist Peter Grant said.

Meanwhile, China continued its gold purchases for the fourth consecutive month in February, with its reserves rising to 73.61M troy oz valued at $208.64B from 73.45M valued at $206.53B at the end of January, according to data from the People's Bank of China.

"The PBOC's purchases are an important factor underpinning gold, so a continuation of its buying in February could help to build further strength behind the gold price," Kinese Money analyst Frank Watson said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for March delivery ended +2.4% at $2,904.70/oz this week, and front-month March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) closed +4.2% to $32.548/oz this week; on Friday, gold fell 0.4% and silver slid 1.5%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Newmont (NEM) expanded its position as the world's top gold producing company last year, amassing 6.7M oz, according to Bloomberg research, thanks to added output from its 2023 takeover of Australia's Newcrest Mining.

Barrick Gold (GOLD) was the runner-up with 3.9M oz produced in 2024, but its lead over Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) narrowed after output fell due to operational setbacks; Agnico Eagle threatens to overtake its bigger rival if Barrick's mine complex in Mali remains closed this year.

The next five names on the list of last year's top producers: Russia's Polyus, AngloGold Ashanti (AU), Zijin Mining (OTCPK:ZIJMF), Gold Fields (GFI) and Kinross Gold (KGC).

All NYSE-traded names show strong YTD gains: Newmont (NEM) +17.9%, Barrick (GOLD) +20%, Agnico Eagle (AEM) +26.3%, AngloGold (AU) +36.4%, Gold Fields (GFI) +52.9%, Kinross (KGC) +20.3%.

data.title: Hims & Hers abandons dermatology business Apostrophe it paid $190M for
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) shut down Apostrophe on Friday, the skincare business it acquired in 2019 for $190M.

A message at the top of Apostrophe's website says, "Apostrophe is being discontinued and all current subscriptions will be canceled as of March 7th, 2025," adding that the platform will go dark in 30 days.

Hims & Hers told Business Insider that the decision was made "to simplify our dermatology products and operations into one seamless experience for our customers."  

The company offers a minoxidil tablet for hair loss, as well as various skin care creams for both men and women.

The shuttering of Apostrophe comes shortly after a March 5 decision by a federal judge to deny an injunction request from a compounding medication trade group to allow it to continue making versions of Eli Lilly's (LLY) tirzepatide, the active ingredient in the weight loss drug Zepbound and type 2 diabetes med Mounjaro.

Hims & Hers markets a compounded version of Novo Nordisk's (NVO) semaglutide, which is found in Wegovy (weight loss) and Ozempic (diabetes). The same organization, the Outsourcing Facilities Association, has also filed suit aiming to protect sales of compounded semaglutide. 

Compounded versions of drugs under patent protection are permitted if that drug is in shortage. However, the U.S. FDA has determined that all four brand-name drugs are no longer in shortage.

data.title: Vaccinex plans to delist common stock from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Vaccinex (OTC:VCNX) on Friday announced that it has notified the Nasdaq of its decision to delist the company’s shares of common stock from Nasdaq.
  • Trading in the common stock on Nasdaq has been suspended since December 18, 2024.
  • Vaccinex intends to file a Form 25 with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or about March 17, 2025 to remove its common stock from listing on Nasdaq.
  • Source: Press Release
